enriched_points.expanded_content: Toxic intermediates in metabolic pathways can inhibit enzyme function, leading to inefficient reactions and potential cellular damage, as highlighted in the interplay of toxicity regulation in prokaryotic pathways.
Current optimization methods often struggle to scale effectively from laboratory settings to industrial applications, limiting their practical utility in large-scale metabolic engineering projects.

Effective metabolic pathway optimization requires collaboration across disciplines such as biochemistry, bioinformatics, and systems biology to integrate diverse expertise and enhance overall outcomes.
